model_overview.project_name: Explainable Insurance Claim Severity ML System
model_overview.primary_objective: Predict insurance claim severity using engineered numerical and categorical features.

out_of_scope_use_cases: fully automated decision-making without human review
production insurance underwriting without additional validation
medical, legal, or life-critical decision support

explanation_behavior: raw sparse input is aligned to expected raw training features
missing features are imputed with serving defaults
input is transformed through the saved feature pipeline
prediction is generated using the selected best model
top 10 SHAP feature contributions are returned

risks: model performance may degrade under distribution shift
explanations indicate feature contribution, not causality
synthetic monitoring baselines may produce unstable statistical warnings
ethical_considerations.production_readiness_note: This system demonstrates production-style architecture, but a real production rollout would require stronger governance, fairness analysis, and historical-baseline monitoring.

known_limitations: baseline drift reference is currently synthetic rather than historical
feature contribution labels are generic transformed feature indices
fairness assessment is not yet implemented
robust adversarial input testing is not yet implemented
